provenance.source_corpus: Long-term functionality of woody debris structures for forest-floor small mammals on clearcuts

nodes.name: Retention of excess woody debris in piles and windrows

source_spans.text: Retention of excess woody debris in piles and windrows provides habitat for forest- ﬂoor small mammals

nodes.name: Abundance of forest-floor small mammal community

source_spans.text: abundance, species richness, and diversity were increased or maintained in the debris structures over the 12-year period

nodes.name: Species richness of forest-floor small mammal community

source_spans.text: abundance, species richness, and diversity were increased or maintained in the debris structures over the 12-year period

nodes.name: Species diversity of forest-floor small mammal community

source_spans.text: abundance, species richness, and diversity were increased or maintained in the debris structures over the 12-year period
Woody debris structures provided habitat on new clearcuts for M. gapperi at comparable or higher abundance than in uncut forest
Mean abundance of M. longicaudus was consistently higher (2.8 to 3.5 times) in piles and windrows than in sites with dispersed woody debris
Mean abundance of P. maniculatus was similar among treatment sites and consistent over time
Mean abundance of total small mammals was consistently higher (1.8 to 2.4 times) in piles and windrows than dispersed or forest sites
Reproduction and survival followed the pattern of abundance for the major species
Reproduction and survival followed the pattern of abundance for the major species

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:913590d99b96
edges.original_sentence: Our results did not support H 1 as abundance, species richness, and diversity were increased or maintained in the debris structures over the 12-year period.
